page: Microsoft Azure E192s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 server family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated x86_64 architecture with 192 vCPUs derived from 96 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1D cache. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for external disk attachments.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 10% for both small-block cached reads at 15010.89 GB/sec and large-block uncached reads at 688.47 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform exceptionally well, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 36.5 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression achieving 1008.9 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the platform demonstrates strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds are average to weak due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a resource density perspective, the E192s_v7 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound enterprise workloads by consolidating massive databases and memory-intensive applications onto a single virtualized node. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external storage configurations for disk-heavy operations and limiting its utility for real-time LLM text generation. This server is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, complex data simulations, and enterprise-grade ETL pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E192s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it excels in memory-intensive and multi-threaded compute tasks, scoring in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng, database operations, and memory bandwidth. The server is highly efficient for consolidating massive in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and complex enterprise ETL pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E192s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E192s_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for enterprise database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring massive RAM capacity and top-tier multi-core processing for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: Features 192 dedicated vCPUs and 1536.0 GB of RAM on x86_64 architecture.
Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with a 480 MB L3 cache.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
Lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on Premium Storage capabilities.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and enterprise ETL workloads.
